adrs.title: Agent spec 'model:' field semantics
adrs.context: Agent spec YAMLs today may declare a model field with or without a provider prefix. packages/pi-behavior-monitors/index.ts:1091-1097 implements parseModelSpec such that bare model ids (e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-6') default to provider='anthropic'. This structural pin means monitor classify calls attempt Anthropic-direct authentication regardless of the main session's configured provider. The user configured OpenRouter as the sole credential source, and monitors failed with 'Expected either apiKey or authToken' because the Anthropic SDK received undefined credentials. The bundled classifier agent YAMLs (hedge, commit-hygiene, fragility, work-quality, unauthorized-action) all use bare model ids and are all affected. The question is what 'model:' means in an agent spec at the framework level.
adrs.decision: Option (iii): a bare model id in an agent spec is resolved against the current session's configured provider via ExtensionContext. If ExtensionContext cannot supply a current-provider/current-model binding, the dispatch errors with a clear message naming the missing context. Fully-qualified model specs (provider/model) continue to pin the provider regardless of session. Pinning is explicit, not a default.

options_considered.label: (i) bare = inherit current session model
options_considered.description: Bare model id silently inherits whatever the parent pi session is running. No error if session has no model — monitors use whatever is ambient.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Implicit. Agent YAMLs become opaque about what they actually run. Debugging 'why did this monitor use that model' becomes harder. But monitors always follow user's session intent automatically.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Silent inheritance violates the spec-loop invariant of explicit provenance; agents should be reproducible from their spec

options_considered.label: (ii) bare = parse error
options_considered.description: Every agent YAML must declare a fully-qualified provider/model. Bundled classifier YAMLs become unrunnable without user override or package-side commitment to one provider.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Maximum explicitness. Forces users to take a stance per spec. But makes bundled YAMLs non-portable across providers and forces pi-behavior-monitors to pick a provider to ship with, which moves the pin instead of removing it.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Moves the pin rather than resolving it; forces package authors to prefer one provider over another at ship time

options_considered.label: (iii) bare = ExtensionContext lookup with explicit error
options_considered.description: Bare model id triggers a runtime lookup against ExtensionContext for the current session's provider and model. If ExtensionContext exposes 'currentModel', that is used. If not, dispatch errors naming the missing binding. Fully-qualified specs continue to pin explicitly.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Requires that ExtensionContext expose 'currentModel' to extensions. If pi-agent-core does not expose this yet, the change widens into the pi-agent-core surface. The trade is: framework becomes more honest about what's available at the boundary, and monitors naturally follow the user's session without any bundled-YAML hardcoding.

consequences: pi-behavior-monitors' classifyViaAgent must read the current session model from ExtensionContext at dispatch time, not from the agent YAML
parseModelSpec semantics change — it no longer returns (provider='anthropic') as a default; bare specs route through ExtensionContext lookup
The five bundled classifier YAMLs can remain with bare model ids and will now follow whatever provider the user has configured
If ExtensionContext does not currently expose currentModel, that becomes a prerequisite unblocker — opens a scope question into pi-agent-core that must be verified before implementation
Workflow per-step provider override (currently a known gap in pi-workflows dispatch.ts) becomes the symmetric problem for workflow agents; same resolution applies

adrs.title: Thinking-seam enforcement for forced-toolChoice dispatch
adrs.context: Agent spec YAMLs can declare thinking: on, but packages/pi-behavior-monitors/index.ts:1235-1248 does not forward thinking to the complete() call. The comment at line 1231 acknowledges that the Anthropic API rejects thinking + forced toolChoice, so thinking must stay off when the dispatch uses a phantom-tool verdict shape. All five bundled classifier YAMLs (hedge, commit-hygiene, fragility, work-quality, unauthorized-action) declare thinking: on despite the silent drop. The config is currently dead — either the YAMLs are wrong, or the classify path is wrong. The framework does not enforce consistency between spec-declared thinking and dispatch-time behavior.
adrs.decision: executeAgent in pi-jit-agents (once parseModelSpec and classify dispatch move there per ADR-0003) enforces thinking=off whenever toolChoice.type === 'tool' is present in the dispatch options. If the agent spec declares thinking: on in combination with forced toolChoice, the framework emits a warning naming the agent and the incompatibility, and proceeds with thinking disabled. Free-form (non-forced) dispatches continue to forward thinking per the spec. The forced-toolChoice path never silently drops thinking without notification.

options_considered.label: (i) forward thinking unconditionally
options_considered.description: executeAgent forwards the compiled spec's thinking field to complete() in every path. Anthropic API rejects the combination at the provider boundary, surfacing the error to the caller.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Honors the spec literally. But failures happen at the transport layer with cryptic errors; agent authors cannot tell from the spec whether it will work at runtime.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Makes the agent spec unreliable — a valid-looking spec fails at dispatch with a transport error

options_considered.label: (ii) silently drop thinking when forced toolChoice
options_considered.description: Current behavior. Classify path ignores thinking. No warning.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Works. But the YAML's thinking field becomes meaningless in the context where it matters most.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Silent drops violate explicit-provenance discipline; future authors writing new classifier YAMLs will repeat the same dead-config mistake

options_considered.label: (iii) enforce thinking=off with warning when forced toolChoice
options_considered.description: executeAgent checks for forced toolChoice; if present and thinking is on, logs a warning naming the agent and disables thinking for this dispatch.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Explicit. Agents authors see the warning and can correct the YAML. Does not break working configurations. Slightly more code at the boundary.

consequences: executeAgent gains a pre-dispatch check that inspects toolChoice and the compiled spec's thinking field
The five bundled classifier YAMLs should be corrected to thinking: off for cleanliness, separately from the enforcement change
Any future agent that combines forced toolChoice with thinking: on will be flagged loudly rather than silently mis-executing
Warning channel must exist at the framework boundary — requires either a logger injected via DispatchContext or a structured diagnostic emitted alongside JitAgentResult

adrs.title: parseModelSpec ownership at the execute boundary
adrs.context: parseModelSpec lives in packages/pi-behavior-monitors/index.ts today. It defaults bare model ids to provider='anthropic', pinning the monitor classify path to Anthropic regardless of session configuration. The same function logic (model-id parsing and provider selection) belongs at the execute boundary, which pi-jit-agents now owns per docs/planning/jit-agents-spec.md §2. Keeping it in pi-behavior-monitors creates duplication (issue-043) and embeds policy in the wrong layer. pi-workflows has the same structural question for its agent-step dispatch but with different currently-hidden behavior.
adrs.decision: Move parseModelSpec into pi-jit-agents as part of the executeAgent boundary. Remove the duplicate from pi-behavior-monitors. Update the consumer's classifyViaAgent to call the pi-jit-agents API. Audit pi-workflows for any implicit model-spec parsing in step dispatch; if found, consolidate there too. The function's new location must accommodate the policy decision from ADR-0001 (ExtensionContext lookup for bare specs) and the enforcement from ADR-0002 (thinking-seam).

options_considered.label: (i) keep parseModelSpec in pi-behavior-monitors
options_considered.description: Leave the function where it is. Fix only the anthropic-pin default.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Minimal change. But the function is structurally in the wrong layer; the next consumer (pi-workflows during migration) would need its own copy.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Leaves duplication across consumers and embeds execute-boundary policy in a consumer

options_considered.label: (ii) move to pi-jit-agents execute boundary
options_considered.description: Export parseModelSpec (or its renamed successor) from pi-jit-agents at the execute surface. Both consumers import it. The function reads ExtensionContext for bare-spec resolution per ADR-0001.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Correct boundary. Requires a consumer-migration step to remove the duplicate. Couples with ADR-0001 (ExtensionContext access) and ADR-0002 (thinking-seam).

options_considered.label: (iii) move to pi-project SDK
options_considered.description: Model resolution becomes a pi-project concern.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Wrong package — pi-project is project state, not runtime execution. Rejected on boundary grounds.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Violates the layered package model in jit-agents-spec §2

consequences: pi-jit-agents gains a model-resolution surface at executeAgent — either as a helper function or inlined into the dispatch path
pi-behavior-monitors' classifyViaAgent shrinks — it calls executeAgent with a compiled spec and an ExtensionContext, no longer doing its own provider resolution
pi-workflows' agent-step dispatch audits for equivalent implicit parsing and consolidates there too
Requires ADR-0001 resolved before implementation, because the bare-spec semantics determine what parseModelSpec's new contract is
